content.content:
########## roles/users/tasks/main.yml ##########
---
# users — ensure the `ai` operator user exists with passwordless sudo and the CI
# public key(s). PUBLIC keys only; private keys live in 1Password/CI (plan §4).

- name: Ensure managed user exists
  ansible.builtin.user:
    name: "{{ managed_user }}"
    shell: /bin/bash
    create_home: true
    state: present

- name: Grant passwordless sudo to the managed user
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    # Matches the existing file on the box byte-for-byte (filename, content, mode) so a
    # re-apply is a no-op instead of leaving a duplicate 90-ai file (confirmed live 2026-06-23).
    dest: "/etc/sudoers.d/{{ managed_user }}"
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0440"
    validate: "visudo -cf %s"          # never write a broken sudoers file
    content: "{{ managed_user }} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL\n"

- name: Install authorized public keys for the managed user
  ansible.posix.authorized_key:
    user: "{{ managed_user }}"
    key: "{{ item }}"
    state: present
    exclusive: false                   # never wipe keys we don't manage (lockout risk)
  loop: "{{ users_authorized_keys }}"
  when: users_authorized_keys | length > 0

########## roles/users/defaults/main.yml ##########
---
managed_user: "ai"
# PUBLIC keys only. Leave empty until the CI deploy key exists; an empty list is a no-op,
# so this role will never strip existing keys and lock anyone out.
users_authorized_keys: []

########## roles/dev_tools/tasks/main.yml ##########
---
# dev_tools — install the NON-apt host tools, version-pinned, so a rebuild reproduces them
# (Go, supabase, the global npm CLIs, codex, and the cloudflared symlink). Each task is guarded
# by a version check so applying to the live box is a no-op. Recovery gap R2
# (docs/RECOVERY-GAP-PLAN.md). Authenticating these tools after a rebuild stays a manual step.

# ---- Go toolchain (tarball -> /usr/local/go, symlinked into /usr/local/bin) ----
- name: Check installed Go version
  ansible.builtin.command: /usr/local/go/bin/go version
  register: dt_go
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  check_mode: false

- name: Install Go {{ go_version }}
  when: ('go' ~ go_version) not in (dt_go.stdout | default(''))
  block:
    - name: Download Go {{ go_version }}
      ansible.builtin.get_url:
        url: "https://go.dev/dl/go{{ go_version }}.linux-{{ dev_tools_arch }}.tar.gz"
        dest: "/tmp/go{{ go_version }}.tar.gz"
        mode: "0644"
    - name: Remove any previous Go
      ansible.builtin.file:
        path: /usr/local/go
        state: absent
    - name: Extract Go to /usr/local
      ansible.builtin.unarchive:
        src: "/tmp/go{{ go_version }}.tar.gz"
        dest: /usr/local
        remote_src: true
    - name: Symlink go and gofmt into /usr/local/bin
      ansible.builtin.file:
        src: "/usr/local/go/bin/{{ item }}"
        dest: "/usr/local/bin/{{ item }}"
        state: link
      loop: [go, gofmt]

# ---- supabase CLI (official .deb) ----
- name: Check installed supabase version
  ansible.builtin.command: supabase --version
  register: dt_sb
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  check_mode: false

- name: Install supabase CLI from the official .deb
  ansible.builtin.apt:
    deb: "https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v{{ supabase_version }}/supabase_{{ supabase_version }}_linux_{{ dev_tools_arch }}.deb"
  when: supabase_version not in (dt_sb.stdout | default(''))

# ---- global npm CLIs (idempotent) ----
- name: Install global npm CLIs (pinned)
  community.general.npm:
    name: "{{ item.name }}"
    version: "{{ item.version }}"
    global: true
    state: present
  loop: "{{ dev_tools_npm }}"
  loop_control:
    label: "{{ item.name }}@{{ item.version }}"

# ---- codex (standalone, via @openai/codex; guarded so prod is a no-op) ----
- name: Check installed codex version
  ansible.builtin.command: codex --version
  register: dt_codex
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  check_mode: false

- name: Install codex {{ codex_version }}
  community.general.npm:
    name: "@openai/codex"
    version: "{{ codex_version }}"
    global: true
    state: present
  when: codex_version not in (dt_codex.stdout | default(''))

# ---- cloudflared symlink (binary is apt-installed by the packages role to /usr/bin) ----
- name: Symlink /usr/local/bin/cloudflared -> /usr/bin/cloudflared (used by Tunnel 1 unit)
  ansible.builtin.file:
    src: /usr/bin/cloudflared
    dest: /usr/local/bin/cloudflared
    state: link
    force: true

########## roles/dev_tools/defaults/main.yml ##########
---
# Non-apt tools, version-pinned to what's live on hetz (2026-06-24). Recovery gap R2.
# Bump these when the tools are upgraded so a rebuild reproduces the current versions.
dev_tools_arch: "amd64"          # x86_64
go_version: "1.26.4"
supabase_version: "2.98.2"
codex_version: "0.141.0"         # @openai/codex (standalone, installs under /opt/codex)
# Global npm CLIs (idempotent via community.general.npm). codex is handled separately (guarded).
dev_tools_npm:
  - { name: "@anthropic-ai/claude-code", version: "2.1.160" }
  - { name: "@google/gemini-cli", version: "0.44.1" }
  - { name: "corepack", version: "0.34.7" }
  - { name: "npm-check-updates", version: "22.2.1" }

content.text: Now let me read the `base` role and the `backrest_watchdog` role (my template for the new `dns_watchdog` role):

input.command:
cd /c/repos/ansible/ansible
echo "########## roles/base/tasks/main.yml ##########"; cat roles/base/tasks/main.yml
echo; echo "########## backrest_watchdog tasks ##########"; cat roles/backrest_watchdog/tasks/main.yml
echo; echo "########## backrest_watchdog handlers ##########"; cat roles/backrest_watchdog/handlers/main.yml 2>/dev/null
echo; echo "########## backrest_watchdog files list ##########"; ls -la roles/backrest_watchdog/files/
echo; echo "########## backrest_watchdog defaults ##########"; cat roles/backrest_watchdog/defaults/main.yml 2>/dev/null

content.content:
########## roles/base/tasks/main.yml ##########
---
# base — apt packages, timezone, unattended-upgrades, journald limits. Non-disruptive.

- name: Read current timezone
  ansible.builtin.command: timedatectl show -p Timezone --value
  register: base_current_tz
  changed_when: false
  check_mode: false      # a genuine read; safe to run in --check (plan §4a)

- name: Set system timezone (builtin only — no extra collections)
  ansible.builtin.command: "timedatectl set-timezone {{ host_timezone }}"
  when:
    - host_timezone | length > 0
    - base_current_tz.stdout != host_timezone
  changed_when: true

- name: Ensure base packages are present
  ansible.builtin.apt:
    name: "{{ base_packages }}"
    state: present
    update_cache: true
    cache_valid_time: 3600

- name: Configure unattended-upgrades (enable automatic security updates)
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    dest: /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgrades
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
    content: |
      // Managed by Ansible (base role). Do not edit by hand.
      APT::Periodic::Update-Package-Lists "1";
      APT::Periodic::Unattended-Upgrade "1";
  when: base_unattended_upgrades | bool

- name: Ensure journald drop-in directory exists
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d
    state: directory
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0755"

- name: Cap journald disk usage
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    dest: /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/00-ansible-limits.conf
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
    content: |
      # Managed by Ansible (base role).
      [Journal]
      SystemMaxUse={{ base_journald_max_use }}
  notify: Restart journald

########## backrest_watchdog tasks ##########
---
# backrest_watchdog — installs the systemd timer that self-heals the backup agent when a host
# Docker restart leaves it holding a stale /var/run/docker.sock handle (plan §3.4, the June
# 2026 outage that silently broke DB backups for 4 days).
#
# The script + units are vendored VERBATIM from the live box (captured 2026-06-23), whose
# canonical source is the backrest-wiz repo (hetzner-producer). Deploying them byte-for-byte
# makes a rebuild reproduce the exact watchdog and keeps re-applies idempotent. Keep in sync
# with that repo — do not "improve" these files here or they'll drift from prod.

- name: Install backrest dump watchdog script
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: backrest-dump-watchdog.sh
    dest: /usr/local/bin/backrest-dump-watchdog.sh
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0755"

- name: Install backrest watchdog service unit
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: backrest-dump-watchdog.service
    dest: /etc/systemd/system/backrest-dump-watchdog.service
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
  notify: Reload systemd

- name: Install backrest watchdog timer unit
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: backrest-dump-watchdog.timer
    dest: /etc/systemd/system/backrest-dump-watchdog.timer
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
  notify: Reload systemd

- name: Flush handlers so the timer unit exists before we enable it
  ansible.builtin.meta: flush_handlers

- name: Enable and start the backrest watchdog timer
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: backrest-dump-watchdog.timer
    enabled: true
    state: started

########## backrest_watchdog handlers ##########
---
- name: Reload systemd
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    daemon_reload: true

########## backrest_watchdog files list ##########
total 14
drwxr-xr-x 1 ahazan2 197121    0 Jun 23 17:12 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 ahazan2 197121    0 Jun 23 17:13 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 ahazan2 197121  234 Jun 23 17:12 backrest-dump-watchdog.service
-rwxr-xr-x 1 ahazan2 197121 2096 Jun 23 17:12 backrest-dump-watchdog.sh
-rw-r--r-- 1 ahazan2 197121  180 Jun 23 17:12 backrest-dump-watchdog.timer

########## backrest_watchdog defaults ##########
---
# The script + units are vendored verbatim from the live box (canonical: backrest-wiz repo),
# so there are no tunables here — changing schedule/behaviour means updating those files to
# match prod, not overriding a variable.

aiTitle: Review documentation and implement handoff requirements

content.text:
I have everything I need. Decisions locked:
- **dns-watchdog** → capture as a new Phase 1 role (verbatim).
- **`/usr/local/sbin` blind spot** → extend `discover-software.sh` + regenerate baseline.
- **socks5** → remove (disable + delete unit).
- **nova** → remove.
- **nasbridge** → remove, after chowning its stray `/usr/local` tools (supabase + gemini-cli) to **root**.

cd /c/repos/ansible/ansible
mkdir -p roles/dns_watchdog/files roles/dns_watchdog/tasks roles/dns_watchdog/handlers roles/dns_watchdog/defaults
# Fetch the three live files byte-exact via base64, decode into the role's files/
for f in "fix-dns-if-broken.sh:/usr/local/sbin/fix-dns-if-broken.sh" \
         "dns-watchdog.service:/etc/systemd/system/dns-watchdog.service" \
         "dns-watchdog.timer:/etc/systemd/system/dns-watchdog.timer"; do
  name="${f%%:*}"; path="${f##*:}"
  ssh -o BatchMode=yes vps "sudo base64 -w0 $path" | base64 -d > "roles/dns_watchdog/files/$name"
  echo "--- $name ($(wc -c < roles/dns_watchdog/files/$name) bytes) ---"
done
echo "=== sanity: show fetched files ==="
for n in fix-dns-if-broken.sh dns-watchdog.service dns-watchdog.timer; do echo "##### $n #####"; cat "roles/dns_watchdog/files/$n"; echo "[EOF]"; done

##### fix-dns-if-broken.sh #####
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

IFACE="$(ip route | awk '/default/ {print $5; exit}')"

if getent ahosts google.com >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
   getent ahosts cloudflare.com >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  exit 0
fi

logger -t dns-watchdog "DNS broken. Reapplying settings."

systemctl restart systemd-resolved
resolvectl flush-caches || true

if getent ahosts google.com >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  logger -t dns-watchdog "DNS repaired."
else
  logger -t dns-watchdog "DNS repair failed — manual intervention needed."
  exit 1
fi
[EOF]
##### dns-watchdog.service #####
[Unit]
Description=Repair DNS if external resolution is broken

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/fix-dns-if-broken.sh
[EOF]
##### dns-watchdog.timer #####
[Unit]
Description=Run DNS watchdog every 5 minutes

[Timer]
OnBootSec=2min
OnUnitActiveSec=5min
AccuracySec=30s
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
[EOF]

content.text: Verbatim files captured (LF preserved). Now the role tasks, handler, defaults, and README:

input.content:
---
# dns_watchdog — installs the systemd timer that self-heals host DNS when external resolution
# breaks (the counterpart to dns_hardening's FallbackDNS, born of the May 2026 DNS cascade
# outage — AGENTS §13). Every 5 minutes a oneshot service tests real resolution and, if broken,
# restarts systemd-resolved + flushes caches.
#
# The script + units are vendored VERBATIM from the live box (captured 2026-06-25 during the
# gap re-audit). It was running on prod but had never been wrapped in a role, so a rebuild would
# NOT have reproduced it — closing that recovery gap. Deploying byte-for-byte keeps re-applies
# idempotent; do not "improve" these files here or they'll drift from prod.

- name: Install the DNS-repair script
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: fix-dns-if-broken.sh
    dest: /usr/local/sbin/fix-dns-if-broken.sh
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0755"

- name: Install dns-watchdog service unit
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: dns-watchdog.service
    dest: /etc/systemd/system/dns-watchdog.service
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
  notify: Reload systemd

- name: Install dns-watchdog timer unit
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: dns-watchdog.timer
    dest: /etc/systemd/system/dns-watchdog.timer
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
  notify: Reload systemd

- name: Flush handlers so the timer unit exists before we enable it
  ansible.builtin.meta: flush_handlers

- name: Enable and start the dns-watchdog timer
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: dns-watchdog.timer
    enabled: true
    state: started

input.content:
# dns_watchdog

Host-side DNS self-heal. Installs a systemd timer (`dns-watchdog.timer`, every 5 min) that runs
a oneshot service (`dns-watchdog.service`) which tests real external resolution
(`google.com`/`cloudflare.com` via `getent ahosts`) and, if it's broken, restarts
`systemd-resolved` and flushes caches — logging the outcome to the journal (`-t dns-watchdog`).

This is the active counterpart to [`dns_hardening`](../dns_hardening/README.md) (which sets the
**static** `FallbackDNS`). Both exist because of the **May 2026 DNS cascade outage** (AGENTS §13),
where host `systemd-resolved` broke and took every tunnel/oauth2-proxy container down.

## Why it's a role now

The script + units were running on `hetz` but had only ever been set up by hand — they were **not
in any role**, so a "rebuild everything" would have silently dropped the watchdog. The gap
re-audit on **2026-06-25** caught it (it hid in `/usr/local/sbin`, which the software-drift check
didn't scan until that audit also fixed `bin/discover-software.sh`). The files here are vendored
**verbatim from the live box** for byte-idempotency — do not edit them to "improve" behaviour;
update them only to track what's actually deployed.

## What it manages

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/usr/local/sbin/fix-dns-if-broken.sh` | the repair script (test → restart resolved → flush) |
| `/etc/systemd/system/dns-watchdog.service` | oneshot that runs the script |
| `/etc/systemd/system/dns-watchdog.timer` | fires it `OnBootSec=2min`, then every 5 min |

Phase 1 (non-disruptive): only restarts `systemd-resolved` when DNS is **already broken**, so a
routine apply is a no-op on a healthy box.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
# site.yml — the single entrypoint that applies the host-layer roles to the Hetzner box.
#
# Roles are split into PHASES (plan §9) and tagged so CI / operators can run a subset:
#   ansible-playbook playbooks/site.yml --tags phase1
#   ansible-playbook playbooks/site.yml --tags firewall -l scratch -e enable_phase2=true
#
# Phase 2 (risky) roles are double-gated: by `enable_phase2` (default false) AND by their
# own per-role guard. They CANNOT touch prod until both are explicitly enabled (§4a, §9).

- name: Host-layer configuration
  # Defaults to the prod group; override for the scratch host with
  # `-e target_hosts=scratch` (plan §8.3 rebuild-and-diff). CI/normal runs are unaffected.
  hosts: "{{ target_hosts | default('hetzner') }}"
  become: true
  gather_facts: true

  pre_tasks:
    - name: Fail fast if a phase2 role is requested without explicit opt-in
      ansible.builtin.assert:
        that:
          - enable_phase2 | bool
        fail_msg: >-
          Phase 2 (risky) roles require `enable_phase2=true`. Prove them on the scratch
          host first (plan §8.3, §9). Refusing to run firewall/docker/cloudflared on prod blind.
        success_msg: "Phase 2 explicitly enabled — proceeding (hopefully on scratch or in a maintenance window)."
      when: "'phase2' in ansible_run_tags or 'firewall' in ansible_run_tags or 'docker' in ansible_run_tags or 'cloudflared_coolify' in ansible_run_tags"
      tags: [always]

  roles:
    # ---- Phase 1: non-disruptive (additive/low-risk) ----
    # apt_repos FIRST so base/packages/docker can install from the vendor repos (recovery R1).
    - role: apt_repos
      tags: [phase1, apt_repos]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: motd
      tags: [phase1, motd]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: base
      tags: [phase1, base]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: packages
      tags: [phase1, packages]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: dev_tools
      tags: [phase1, dev_tools]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: users
      tags: [phase1, users]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: ssh_hardening
      tags: [phase1, ssh_hardening]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: dns_hardening
      tags: [phase1, dns_hardening]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    - role: backrest_watchdog
      tags: [phase1, backrest_watchdog]
      when: enable_phase1 | bool

    # ---- Phase 2: risky live-service roles (can take the box down) ----
    - role: coolify
      tags: [phase2, coolify]
      when: enable_phase2 | bool

    - role: firewall
      tags: [phase2, firewall]
      when: enable_phase2 | bool

    - role: docker
      tags: [phase2, docker]
      when: enable_phase2 | bool

    - role: cron_glue
      tags: [phase2, cron_glue]
      when: enable_phase2 | bool

    - role: cloudflared_coolify
      tags: [phase2, cloudflared_coolify]
      when: enable_phase2 | bool

lines: tags: [phase1, dns_hardening]
when: enable_phase1 | bool

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# discover-software.sh — print a NORMALIZED inventory of every piece of software on the host,
# for the recovery software-coverage check (gap R6). Run on the box; makes ZERO changes.
#
# The output is a sorted, stable list with one-line "CATEGORY  name[=version]" entries. It is
# compared against the committed baseline (docs/baseline-software.txt) by the software-drift CI
# job: anything on the box that isn't in the baseline = software installed outside Ansible.
#
#   ssh vps 'sudo bash -s' < bin/discover-software.sh
set -uo pipefail

# Manually-installed apt packages (not pulled in as deps, not part of the base image)
comm -23 <(apt-mark showmanual | sort) \
         <(gzip -dc /var/log/installer/initial-status.gz 2>/dev/null | sed -n 's/^Package: //p' | sort) \
  | sed 's/^/APT  /'

# Binaries / scripts dropped into /usr/local/bin (the non-apt tools + glue scripts)
ls -1 /usr/local/bin 2>/dev/null | sort | sed 's/^/BIN  /'

# Global npm packages (name@version)
if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  npm ls -g --depth=0 --parseable 2>/dev/null \
    | sed 's#.*/node_modules/##' | grep -v '^/' | grep . | sort \
    | sed 's/^/NPM  /'
fi

# Snap packages
if command -v snap >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  snap list 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR>1{print $1}' | sort | sed 's/^/SNAP /'
fi

lines: # Binaries / scripts dropped into /usr/local/bin (the non-apt tools + glue scripts)

file.content:
---
# users — ensure the `ai` operator user exists with passwordless sudo and the CI
# public key(s). PUBLIC keys only; private keys live in 1Password/CI (plan §4).

- name: Ensure managed user exists
  ansible.builtin.user:
    name: "{{ managed_user }}"
    shell: /bin/bash
    create_home: true
    state: present

- name: Grant passwordless sudo to the managed user
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    # Matches the existing file on the box byte-for-byte (filename, content, mode) so a
    # re-apply is a no-op instead of leaving a duplicate 90-ai file (confirmed live 2026-06-23).
    dest: "/etc/sudoers.d/{{ managed_user }}"
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0440"
    validate: "visudo -cf %s"          # never write a broken sudoers file
    content: "{{ managed_user }} ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL\n"

- name: Install authorized public keys for the managed user
  ansible.posix.authorized_key:
    user: "{{ managed_user }}"
    key: "{{ item }}"
    state: present
    exclusive: false                   # never wipe keys we don't manage (lockout risk)
  loop: "{{ users_authorized_keys }}"
  when: users_authorized_keys | length > 0

input.new_string:
loop: "{{ users_authorized_keys }}"
  when: users_authorized_keys | length > 0

- name: Remove un-owned login users found in the gap re-audit (nova, nasbridge)
  ansible.builtin.user:
    name: "{{ item }}"
    state: absent
    remove: true       # delete the home dir + mail spool too (both are inert/skel-only)
    force: true        # neither is logged in; force handles the edge case if one ever is
  loop: "{{ users_absent }}"
  # dev_tools (runs earlier) re-homes the global CLIs nasbridge owned to root, so no /usr/local
  # file is orphaned by this removal. Idempotent: a no-op once the users are gone (e.g. rebuild).

# ---- Re-home legacy nasbridge-owned tools to root (gap re-audit 2026-06-25) ----
# On prod these system-wide CLIs were owned by the stray `nasbridge` (uid 1001) — the accidental
# identity that once installed them. /usr/local tooling must be root-owned, and this MUST run
# before the users role removes nasbridge, or the files would be left orphaned on a now-dead uid.
# A fresh rebuild installs them as root already, so this is a no-op there and on a corrected box.
- name: Re-home global CLIs under /usr/local to root (supabase + gemini-cli)
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: "{{ item.path }}"
    owner: root
    group: root
    recurse: "{{ item.recurse | default(false) }}"
    state: "{{ item.state | default('file') }}"
  loop:
    - { path: /usr/local/bin/supabase }
    - { path: /usr/local/lib/node_modules/@google/gemini-cli, state: directory, recurse: true }
  failed_when: false   # paths may be absent on a fresh box before npm/.deb installs run

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
# dev_tools — install the NON-apt host tools, version-pinned, so a rebuild reproduces them
# (Go, supabase, the global npm CLIs, codex, and the cloudflared symlink). Each task is guarded
# by a version check so applying to the live box is a no-op. Recovery gap R2
# (docs/RECOVERY-GAP-PLAN.md). Authenticating these tools after a rebuild stays a manual step.

# ---- Go toolchain (tarball -> /usr/local/go, symlinked into /usr/local/bin) ----
- name: Check installed Go version
  ansible.builtin.command: /usr/local/go/bin/go version
  register: dt_go
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  check_mode: false

- name: Install Go {{ go_version }}
  when: ('go' ~ go_version) not in (dt_go.stdout | default(''))
  block:
    - name: Download Go {{ go_version }}
      ansible.builtin.get_url:
        url: "https://go.dev/dl/go{{ go_version }}.linux-{{ dev_tools_arch }}.tar.gz"
        dest: "/tmp/go{{ go_version }}.tar.gz"
        mode: "0644"
    - name: Remove any previous Go
      ansible.builtin.file:
        path: /usr/local/go
        state: absent
    - name: Extract Go to /usr/local
      ansible.builtin.unarchive:
        src: "/tmp/go{{ go_version }}.tar.gz"
        dest: /usr/local
        remote_src: true
    - name: Symlink go and gofmt into /usr/local/bin
      ansible.builtin.file:
        src: "/usr/local/go/bin/{{ item }}"
        dest: "/usr/local/bin/{{ item }}"
        state: link
      loop: [go, gofmt]

# ---- supabase CLI (official .deb) ----
- name: Check installed supabase version
  ansible.builtin.command: supabase --version
  register: dt_sb
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  check_mode: false

- name: Install supabase CLI from the official .deb
  ansible.builtin.apt:
    deb: "https://github.com/supabase/cli/releases/download/v{{ supabase_version }}/supabase_{{ supabase_version }}_linux_{{ dev_tools_arch }}.deb"
  when: supabase_version not in (dt_sb.stdout | default(''))

# ---- global npm CLIs (idempotent) ----
- name: Install global npm CLIs (pinned)
  community.general.npm:
    name: "{{ item.name }}"
    version: "{{ item.version }}"
    global: true
    state: present
  loop: "{{ dev_tools_npm }}"
  loop_control:
    label: "{{ item.name }}@{{ item.version }}"

# ---- codex (standalone, via @openai/codex; guarded so prod is a no-op) ----
- name: Check installed codex version
  ansible.builtin.command: codex --version
  register: dt_codex
  changed_when: false
  failed_when: false
  check_mode: false

- name: Install codex {{ codex_version }}
  community.general.npm:
    name: "@openai/codex"
    version: "{{ codex_version }}"
    global: true
    state: present
  when: codex_version not in (dt_codex.stdout | default(''))

# ---- cloudflared symlink (binary is apt-installed by the packages role to /usr/bin) ----
- name: Symlink /usr/local/bin/cloudflared -> /usr/bin/cloudflared (used by Tunnel 1 unit)
  ansible.builtin.file:
    src: /usr/bin/cloudflared
    dest: /usr/local/bin/cloudflared
    state: link
    force: true

# ---- Tear down the deprecated socks5-home-tunnel service (gap re-audit 2026-06-25) ----
# An SSH SOCKS proxy to a home machine binding 0.0.0.0:1080 — internet-exposed given the host's
# ACCEPT INPUT policy (firewall only locks down port 22). It was found enabled + crash-looping
# (home box unreachable, nothing listening). The plan always said it must stay OFF (§3.5) and the
# owner confirmed it's unused. Stop+disable+remove so a rebuild never recreates it. Idempotent:
# a no-op once the unit is gone (e.g. a fresh box where it never existed).
- name: Disable the deprecated socks5-home-tunnel service
  ansible.builtin.systemd:
    name: socks5-home-tunnel.service
    state: stopped
    enabled: false
  failed_when: false      # unit may already be absent (fresh box); removal below is authoritative

- name: Remove the socks5-home-tunnel unit file
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: /etc/systemd/system/socks5-home-tunnel.service
    state: absent
  notify: Reload systemd

---
# base — apt packages, timezone, unattended-upgrades, journald limits. Non-disruptive.

- name: Read current timezone
  ansible.builtin.command: timedatectl show -p Timezone --value
  register: base_current_tz
  changed_when: false
  check_mode: false      # a genuine read; safe to run in --check (plan §4a)

- name: Set system timezone (builtin only — no extra collections)
  ansible.builtin.command: "timedatectl set-timezone {{ host_timezone }}"
  when:
    - host_timezone | length > 0
    - base_current_tz.stdout != host_timezone
  changed_when: true

- name: Ensure base packages are present
  ansible.builtin.apt:
    name: "{{ base_packages }}"
    state: present
    update_cache: true
    cache_valid_time: 3600

- name: Configure unattended-upgrades (enable automatic security updates)
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    dest: /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgrades
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
    content: |
      // Managed by Ansible (base role). Do not edit by hand.
      APT::Periodic::Update-Package-Lists "1";
      APT::Periodic::Unattended-Upgrade "1";
  when: base_unattended_upgrades | bool

- name: Ensure journald drop-in directory exists
  ansible.builtin.file:
    path: /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d
    state: directory
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0755"

- name: Cap journald disk usage
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    dest: /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/00-ansible-limits.conf
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: "0644"
    content: |
      # Managed by Ansible (base role).
      [Journal]
      SystemMaxUse={{ base_journald_max_use }}
  notify: Restart journald

lines: [Journal]
SystemMaxUse={{ base_journald_max_use }}
notify: Restart journald
+# ---- Tear down the deprecated socks5-home-tunnel service (gap re-audit 2026-06-25) ----
+# An SSH SOCKS proxy to a home machine binding 0.0.0.0:1080 — internet-exposed given the host's
+# ACCEPT INPUT policy (firewall only locks down port 22). It was found enabled + crash-looping
+# (home box unreachable, nothing listening). The plan always said it must stay OFF (§3.5) and the
+# owner confirmed it's unused. Stop+disable+remove so a rebuild never recreates it. Idempotent:
+# a no-op once the unit is gone (e.g. a fresh box where it never existed).
+- name: Disable the deprecated socks5-home-tunnel service
+  ansible.builtin.systemd:
+    name: socks5-home-tunnel.service
+    state: stopped
+    enabled: false
+  failed_when: false      # unit may already be absent (fresh box); removal below is authoritative
+- name: Remove the socks5-home-tunnel unit file
+  ansible.builtin.file:
+    path: /etc/systemd/system/socks5-home-tunnel.service
+    state: absent
+  notify: Reload systemd

content.text:
All gaps are closed, applied to prod, and verified. The doc-only follow-up push will trigger a no-op apply (expected for doc pushes).

## Done — gap re-audit complete and all gaps closed

**Audit results (all three layers):**
| Layer | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Software inventory vs baseline | clean | clean (+ `/usr/local/sbin` now covered) |
| Config drift (phase1 `--check`) | clean | clean |
| Host units / users / glue | **4 gaps** | **all closed** |

**What was found and fixed (commit [`ece6fec`](https://github.com/u2giants/ansible/commit/ece6fec), applied to prod via CI):**

1. **`dns-watchdog`** — a live DNS self-heal timer/service + `/usr/local/sbin/fix-dns-if-broken.sh` that was never in a role (a rebuild would have silently dropped it). Captured verbatim as a new Phase 1 `dns_watchdog` role — **no-op on prod**, so byte-exact.
2. **Audit blind spot** — `discover-software.sh` only scanned `/usr/local/bin`, so the watchdog script in `sbin` was invisible to drift detection. Extended to scan `/usr/local/sbin`; baseline regenerated.
3. **`socks5-home-tunnel.service`** — enabled + crash-looping, bound `0.0.0.0:1080` (internet-exposed under the ACCEPT INPUT policy). You confirmed unused → stopped, disabled, unit removed.
4. **`nova`** (orphan, no home) and **`nasbridge`** (accidental global-install identity) — removed. First re-homed the tools `nasbridge` owned (`supabase`, `gemini-cli`) to **root** so nothing was orphaned. Investigation confirmed nasbridge had **no** tie to popdam (Coolify apps) or the "Gemini MCP bridge" unit (runs as `ai`).

**Verification on the live box:** `nova`/`nasbridge` gone · socks5 unit `not-found` · `supabase` + `gemini-cli` now `root:root` · `dns-watchdog.timer` active+enabled · CI apply `changed=4` · **idempotency re-check `changed=0`**.

The repo once again fully describes the box. The remaining un-managed host timers/crons (`popcrm-*`, `plm-sync`, hiclaw keepers) stay deliberately out-of-scope (app/Coolify-owned, per the discovery doc). The biggest *outstanding* work remains the `backrest-wiz` data-restore integration in its own repo — unchanged by this audit.
